This page contains the table of contents for the document.

pages.subtopic: Pollution, Sustainable Development, and Disaster Management
pages.rationale: This page continues the table of contents.
Contains the end of the table of contents and a large advertisement for a foundation course.
Introductory note explaining the features of the document like infographics and thumbnails.
Contains dense information on the IUCN World Conservation Congress, its resolutions, and India's National Red List Roadmap.

pages.subtopic: IUCN Red List and Green Status of Species
pages.rationale: Detailed explanation of IUCN Red List categories and the newer Green Status of Species assessment framework.

pages.subtopic: IUCN Commissions and Lion Green Status
pages.rationale: Very high density; covers the Kenton Miller Award, SSC, World Heritage Outlook 4, and a detailed comparison between Asiatic and African Lions.

pages.subtopic: UNESCO World Network of Biosphere Reserves (WNBR)
pages.rationale: Covers the MAB programme, designation process, and provides a comprehensive list of Biosphere Reserves in India.

pages.subtopic: Cold Desert BR and Wildlife Protection Act 1972
pages.rationale: Details on the Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve and a summary of the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA) 1972 schedules and institutions.
Extremely dense page covering new ABS regulations, Digital Sequence Information (DSI), Nagoya Protocol, and the Convention on Biological Diversity.
Explains India's legal framework for biodiversity and the Integrated Development of Wildlife Habitats scheme, including a Sloth Bear profile.

pages.subtopic: Project Cheetah and Gandhi Sagar WLS
pages.rationale: Covers the reintroduction of Cheetahs in India, comparison between Asiatic and African Cheetahs, and Gandhi Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary.

pages.subtopic: Tiger Reserves and Supreme Court Directions
pages.rationale: Discusses Supreme Court rulings on tiger safaris and human-wildlife conflict, alongside the legal structure of Tiger Reserves.

pages.subtopic: International Big Cat Alliance (IBCA) and Small Cats
pages.rationale: Details the newly formed IBCA and provides an overview of the status of small cats in India's tiger landscapes.

pages.subtopic: Small Cat Species and Elephant Population Status
pages.rationale: Profiles specific small cats like the Caracal and Fishing Cat, and presents findings from the All India Population Estimation of Elephants.
Very dense content including a detailed comparison of Asian vs African elephants and the history of the Crocodile Conservation Project.
Detailed profiles of Saltwater Crocodiles, Muggers, and Gharials, including their IUCN status, habitats, and unique biological characteristics.

Extremely dense page covering the first comprehensive population estimate, comparative characteristics of Gangetic vs Indus dolphins, IUCN/WPA status, and specific conservation projects like Project Dolphin and NDRC.

Covers the first Pan-India assessment of vultures with specific data on four critically endangered species and their locations. Also introduces the Draft Policy Paper on Camels and population decline drivers.
Detailed breakdown of Indian camel breeds (especially the swimming Kharai camel) and the first chromosome-level genome assembly of the Indian Yak, including Wild Yak conservation status.

pages.subtopic: TN Endangered Species and Forest Amendment Rules 2025
pages.rationale: Details four specific endangered species in Tamil Nadu and introduces the significant Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Amendment Rules 2025 regarding compensatory afforestation.
Explains the legal definition of forest, the Stage I/II clearance procedure, and specific categories of land exempted from the Act, which are high-probability UPSC topics.
Provides objectives and strategies of the Green India Mission and key findings from the India State of Forest Report (ISFR) 2023 regarding forest cover increases.

pages.subtopic: ISFR Terms and UN High Seas Treaty
pages.rationale: Defines critical forest terms (Carbon Stock, Protected Forests) and provides a detailed overview of the BBNJ Agreement (High Seas Treaty) and its pillars.
Detailed explanation of UNCLOS maritime zones (Territorial Sea, EEZ, etc.) and the foundational mandate and 'wise use' principle of the Ramsar Convention.
Dense coverage of Ramsar List criteria, Montreux Record, Wetland City Accreditation, and multiple new initiatives from COP15 like the Victoria Falls Declaration and GWEP.
Lists and describes new Ramsar sites in Rajasthan and Bihar, including Khichan, Menar, and Siliserh, with specific wildlife and geographical details.
Continues the list of new Ramsar sites (Gokul, Udaipur, etc.), discusses Vembanad and Dal lakes, and provides a detailed explanation of coral bleaching and symbiotic relationships.
Covers coral reef types, distribution in India (Coral Triangle), and the SAIME model for sustainable aquaculture in mangrove ecosystems, including mangrove characteristics.
Explains the Island Protection Zone (IPZ) framework, the United Nations Forum on Forests (UNFF), and the outcomes of the Third UN Ocean Conference (UNOC3).
Lists Blue Flag beaches in India, explains the AIM4NatuRe initiative, the Central Asian Mammals Initiative (CAMI), and rare mushroom sightings in Telangana.
Covers the legal definition of Conservation Reserves under WPA 1972, the Hesaraghatta Grassland, the concept of Global Ocean Darkening, and Environmental DNA (eDNA).
Defines Blue Washing and Green Washing, explains functional de-extinction (Dire wolves), and summarizes the first-ever survey of ungulates in India.
Detailed institutional profiles of CITES (including its three appendices) and UPOV, highlighting India's unique sui generis law for plant variety protection.
Covers the IBAT Alliance coalition and provides a comprehensive overview of the National Green Tribunal (NGT), its legislative framework, powers, and governance.
The page contains specific data points regarding global forest cover and India's ranking in forest area and rubber plantations, suitable for factual prelims questions.
High-density content covering the outcomes of COP30, the genesis of UNFCCC, and several new international initiatives like the Belem Mission and TFFF.
Contains detailed information on the Tropical Forest Forever Facility, NCQG targets, and the Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA) measurement framework.
Extremely dense page listing multiple specific global initiatives (FINI, PACT, Belem Health Action Plan) with participants and objectives, ideal for matching or statement-based questions.

pages.subtopic: COP30 Outcomes: Health, Governance, and Food Waste
pages.rationale: Features a table of significant outcomes including the Systematic Observation Impact Bond and the Food Waste Breakthrough, providing clear factual targets.
Dense list of initiatives including the Global Carbon Harvest Coalition (India involved) and Country Platforms for Climate and Nature Finance, which are highly relevant for UPSC.
Covers institutional details of the Green Climate Fund and the India-Sweden LeadIT initiative, both recurring themes in UPSC exams.

pages.subtopic: India's NDCs and GEI Target Rules 2025
pages.rationale: Critical page detailing India's updated NDC targets, Panchamrit commitments, and the newly notified Greenhouse Gases Emission Intensity Rules.

pages.subtopic: Carbon Credit Trading Scheme and India's Emissions
pages.rationale: Provides technical details on the CCTS, India's 4th Biennial Update Report (BUR-4) statistics, and other market mechanisms like PAT and REC.

pages.subtopic: Green Credit Program and Mission LiFE
pages.rationale: Detailed explanation of the Green Credit Program, its methodology, nodal agencies, and its distinction from carbon credits, which is a high-probability topic.

pages.subtopic: Article 6 of Paris Agreement and Montreal Protocol
pages.rationale: Explains the mechanisms of Article 6 (6.2, 6.4, 6.8) and provides a comparison between the Kyoto Protocol and the Paris Agreement.

pages.subtopic: Montreal Protocol and Net Zero Shipping
pages.rationale: Covers the Montreal Protocol, Kigali Amendment, and the new Net-Zero Framework for Global Shipping by the IMO.

pages.subtopic: IMO and Carbon Capture and Utilisation (CCU)
pages.rationale: Details on the International Maritime Organization and the technical pathways for Carbon Capture and Utilisation as per India's R&D roadmap.
Discusses methane as a GHG, the CCAC partnership, and the consequences of glacial loss with specific examples like the Yala Glacier.
Information on India's new research station Maitri II, the Global Reporting Initiative (GRI), and the Net-Zero Banking Alliance.

pages.subtopic: Climate Tipping Points and Arctic Amplification
pages.rationale: High conceptual value regarding climate tipping points identified by IPCC and the scientific factors behind Arctic Amplification.
Explains the nine planetary boundaries and their current status based on the Planetary Health Check 2025.
Comprehensive overview of UNEP, including its genesis, the conventions it hosts, and the major reports it publishes.
Covers the Global Environment Facility (GEF), its location, genesis, and role as a financial mechanism for five major international conventions.
Detailed information on the World Meteorological Organisation (WMO), its climate reports, and various carbon pricing instruments like ETS and carbon taxes.

pages.subtopic: Energy Transition, Climate Risk, and Performance Indices
pages.rationale: Summarizes multiple global indices including the Energy Transition Index, Climate Risk Index, and Climate Change Performance Index with India's rankings.
Dense coverage of the Basel, Rotterdam, and Stockholm (BRS) Conventions, including recent COP outcomes and specific chemical listings.

pages.subtopic: Minamata Convention and Global Plastic Pollution Treaty
pages.rationale: Detailed coverage of the Minamata Convention on Mercury and the evolution of the Global Plastic Pollution Treaty, including key disagreements among nations.
Technical details on microplastics, their types and impacts, alongside India's legislative framework for plastic waste management.

pages.subtopic: Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) and Air Quality Standards
pages.rationale: Comprehensive overview of urban air pollution in India, the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) stages, and a comparison of air quality standards.

pages.subtopic: AQI, CAQM, and Swachh Vayu Sarveshan
pages.rationale: Details on cloud seeding, the National Air Quality Index (AQI) categories, the statutory role of CAQM, and the Swachh Vayu Sarveshan awards.

pages.subtopic: NCAP, Aerosols, and Polluted River Stretches
pages.rationale: Covers the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) targets, aerosol types and their climatic impacts like the Twomey Effect.
Explains the criteria for Polluted River Stretches (PRS), the statutory functions of the CPCB, and the framework for river rejuvenation.
Compares thermal and membrane desalination technologies and introduces the revised Pollution Index-based classification of industries.

pages.subtopic: Revised Industry Classification and Contaminated Sites
pages.rationale: Detailed breakdown of the revised industry classification (Red, Orange, Green, White, Blue) and new rules for managing contaminated sites.

pages.subtopic: Hazardous Waste Amendment Rules and EPR
pages.rationale: Discusses the 2025 Hazardous Waste Amendment Rules, the EPR framework for non-ferrous metals, and global air pollution conferences.

pages.subtopic: GOCWI, River Cities Alliance, and Dumpsite Remediation
pages.rationale: Covers water investment platforms, the River Cities Alliance, and the Dumpsite Remediation Accelerator Programme (DRAP) under SBM-U 2.0.

pages.subtopic: Ground-level Ozone, Heavy Metals, and PFAS
pages.rationale: Technical information on ground-level ozone formation, secondary pollutants, and the toxicity and sources of various heavy metals.

pages.subtopic: Green Firecrackers, FGD, and Taj Trapezium Zone
pages.rationale: Explains the composition and types of green firecrackers, Flue Gas Desulphurisation (FGD) technology, and the Taj Trapezium Zone (TTZ).

pages.subtopic: State of Global Air and Air Quality Life Index
pages.rationale: Summarizes findings from the State of Global Air Report and the Air Quality Life Index regarding the health impacts of pollution.
Reviews 10 years of Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), India's progress, and key global reports monitoring the 2030 Agenda.

The page contains high-value factual data on the SDG India Index (NITI Aayog), the Sevilla Commitment for SDG financing, and specific mechanisms like Debt-for-Development Swaps and the SCALED platform.

This page is extremely dense, covering the evolution of sustainable financing (Monterrey, Doha, Addis Ababa), India's updated NDC and Panchamrita targets, and detailed statistics on the renewable energy sector.
Covers the International Solar Alliance (ISA) institutional details, its 'Towards 1000' strategy, and significant regulatory changes in solar panel efficiency norms (ALMM) by MNRE.
Highly conceptual and factual page detailing the Green Hydrogen Certification Scheme (GHCI), emission intensity standards, and the classification of hydrogen by color/process.

pages.subtopic: National Bioenergy Programme & Green Hydrogen Hubs
pages.rationale: Provides details on the SIGHT programme, designated Green Hydrogen Hubs (ports), and the three components of the National Bioenergy Programme (Waste to Energy, Biomass, Biogas).
Dense content on the Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) programme, the National Policy on Biofuels, and a clear classification of the four generations of biofuels (1G to 4G).
Covers specific alternative fuels including bamboo-based bioethanol, the world's first commercial E-Methanol plant, and India's first cooperative multi-feed CBG plant.
Details the first National Policy on Geothermal Energy, identifying India's geothermal provinces and the potential for electricity generation and direct-use applications.
Discusses geothermal financing mechanisms, the updated ALMM for wind energy, and the draft guidelines for Underground Coal Gasification (UCG).
Covers the ADEETIE scheme for MSME energy efficiency, Coal Gasification incentives (CGFIS), and the Corporate Average Fuel Efficiency (CAFE) norms for passenger cars.
Details the National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF) and the guidelines for setting up 10,000 Bio-Input Resource Centres (BRCs) across India.
Explains the components of natural farming (Beejamrit, Jivamrit, etc.) and provides a comparative analysis between organic and natural farming systems.
Covers the Globally Important Agricultural Heritage Systems (GIAHS) including Indian sites, and the new Model Rules 2025 for tree felling on agricultural land.
Discusses the newly notified Environment Audit Rules 2025, the role of EAs, and recent Supreme Court observations on Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA).
Explains the categorization of projects for Environmental Clearance and the legal/environmental contentions surrounding the Great Nicobar Island Project.
Covers reservoir sedimentation findings, the Dam Safety Act 2021 institutional framework, and the introduction of the Draft Seeds Bill 2025.
Highly dense page with a comparative table of seed legislation and a detailed explanation of the three generations of seeds (Breeder, Foundation, Certified).
Contains critical Supreme Court observations on sand mining replenishment studies and details on the BRICS Land Restoration Partnership and UN-India capacity building.

This page contains multiple distinct frameworks and initiatives like the RECLAIM framework for mines, Green Financing (ARTHA), and international organizations like SEforALL, all highly relevant for UPSC.

pages.subtopic: RPO/RCO, Sustainable Aviation Fuel, Green Municipal Bonds
pages.rationale: Dense with factual details on energy obligations (RPO vs RCO), feedstocks for SAF, and the first certified Green Municipal Bond in India.
Provides technical details on gas hydrates and comprehensive information on IEA membership, mission, and flagship reports.
Covers key organizations like ICRISAT and SECI along with a table of environmental reports, though some content is slightly less dense than previous pages.
Extremely high-signal page covering the Coalition for Disaster Resilient Infrastructure (CDRI), international conferences, and Indian coastal vulnerability initiatives.
Discusses the financing mechanisms for disaster risk reduction in India and the role of UNDRR and the Sendai Framework.
Contains technical explanations of cloudbursts (orographic lift) and glacial lake outburst floods, which are frequent UPSC topics.

pages.subtopic: Tsunami, Oil Spills, MARPOL, Calcium Carbide
pages.rationale: Very dense page covering tsunami wave characteristics, oil spill regulatory frameworks (MARPOL, BUNKER), and the properties of Calcium Carbide.

pages.subtopic: NCMC, RIMES, Weather Derivatives, Oil Spill Tech
pages.rationale: Covers diverse topics including bioremediation for oil spills, statutory status of NCMC, and regional early warning systems like RIMES.

pages.subtopic: Alert Systems (SACHET, EW4All), Twister, Grey Rhino
pages.rationale: Detailed information on India's alert systems and the UN's EW4All initiative, plus conceptual definitions like Grey Rhino events.
High factual density regarding regional names for cyclones, the physics of swell waves (Kallakkadal), and the mandate of INCOIS.

pages.subtopic: Disaster Risk Index (DRI), District Flood Severity Index
pages.rationale: Focuses on specific indices used for disaster assessment; the bottom half is an advertisement, reducing the overall quota.
Contains critical information on the Global Geoparks Network and India's exploration contracts for deep-sea minerals with the ISA.

pages.subtopic: International Seabed Authority (ISA), Bharat Forecast System
pages.rationale: Dense with details on the ISA's mandate and the world's first 6km resolution indigenous weather forecast system (BFS).
Explains the IMD's rainfall categorization and the geophysical phenomenon of True Polar Wander.

pages.subtopic: Reverse Faulting, Pacific Ring of Fire, AMOC
pages.rationale: Provides geological details on faulting, the tectonic features of the Ring of Fire, and the mechanics of AMOC.

pages.subtopic: AMOC Impacts, Delamination Theory, Solar Tornado
pages.rationale: High-value content on the impacts of AMOC slowdown, the new 'Delamination' theory for the Indian plate, and solar phenomena.

pages.subtopic: Deep Ocean, Polar Anticyclone, Thirstwave, Mercator Map
pages.rationale: Dense with diverse geographical concepts including atmospheric 'thirstwaves', polar anticyclones, and map projection distortions.

pages.subtopic: National Centre of Polar and Ocean Research (NCPOR)
pages.rationale: Provides institutional details including genesis, governing body, and key roles in managing India's research stations in Antarctica and the Arctic.
Extremely dense page covering multiple geographical features like Shipki La, Majuli Island, and Barren Island with specific facts on rivers, locations, and status.
High factual density regarding several Indian rivers (Beas, Chenab, Subansiri, etc.), including their origins, tributaries, and associated hydroelectric projects.
Covers significant infrastructure and irrigation projects like Kaleshwaram and Pamban Bridge with relevant geographical and environmental context.
Dense list of international geographical features including Davis Strait, Kamchatka Peninsula, and Drake Passage, suitable for mapping and feature-based questions.
Detailed information on international water bodies like the Aral Sea, Congo River, and Blue Nile, including basin countries and environmental significance.
Lists various volcanic eruptions and earthquakes globally, providing specific locations and geographical context for disaster-related questions.
Discusses countries in the context of specific environmental legal developments (ecocide in Vanuatu) and international treaties (Falepili Union Treaty).
Provides a comprehensive list of protected areas mentioned in the news, useful for identifying locations and regional distributions.
Very high density of information on specific protected areas like Kaziranga and Tsarap Chu, including flora, fauna, and river associations.
Dense content on Keibul Lamjao, Sahyadri, and Similipal, including unique features like Phumdis and specific tribal associations.
Covers Agasthyamala, Dudhwa, and Salkhan Fossil Park, including significant geological facts like stromatolites and the Great Oxidation Event.
Focuses on the expansion and specific designations of the Sundarbans Tiger Reserve.
Detailed profiles of Snow Leopard and Greater One-Horned Rhino, including conservation status across WPA, CITES, and IUCN.
Continuation of Rhino facts and a detailed profile of the Nilgiri Tahr, including its endemic nature and cultural mentions.
Profiles of Dhole and Alpine Musk Deer with specific habitat details and conservation status.
Information on the Indian Grey Wolf and Bonobo, focusing on habitat, behavior, and genetic significance.
Details on the Malabar Grey Hornbill and Greater Flamingo, including breeding grounds and conservation initiatives.

pages.subtopic: Aquatic Species: Dugong and Olive Ridley Turtle
pages.rationale: This page provides detailed conservation status, habitat, and characteristics for the Dugong and Olive Ridley Turtle, including specific legal protections and international conventions (WPA, CMS, CITES, IUCN).

pages.subtopic: Aquatic Species: Asian Giant Softshell Turtle and New Fish Species
pages.rationale: Contains high-density factual information on the Critically Endangered Asian Giant Softshell turtle and two newly discovered endemic fish species in the Western Ghats (Labeo uru and Labeo chekida).
Covers a new beetle species discovery in Arunachal Pradesh and provides significant ecological and distributional facts about Lichens in India, including bioindicator properties.
Discusses the unique biological characteristics of Tardigrades (cryptobiosis) and Cicadas, both of which are relevant for science and environment sections of the prelims.

pages.subtopic: Plant Species: Red Sanders and Wild Banana
pages.rationale: Dense content on Red Sanders (habitat, CITES status, uses) and the discovery of a unique wild banana species in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

pages.subtopic: Plant Species: White Himalayan Lily and Murikooti Pacha
pages.rationale: Details rare and medicinal plant species, their conservation status (CR for Lily), and their therapeutic uses, which are common themes in UPSC species questions.

pages.subtopic: Plant Species: Arogyapacha and Conocarpus Trees
pages.rationale: Features the Arogyapacha plant (Kani tribe association) and the invasive Conocarpus tree, providing a mix of ethnobotany and environmental management facts.

pages.subtopic: Plant Species: Sea Buckthorn and Senna Spectabilis
pages.rationale: Covers Sea Buckthorn's ecological significance in high altitudes and the eradication of the invasive Senna Spectabilis in Kerala.
